name: "Bartholomew Kuma"
intro_text: Pacifista Prototype Mark III Statistics Japanese Name: パシフィスタ Romanized Name: Pashifisuta Official English Name: Pacifista First Appearance: Chapter 505 ; Episode 399 [ 1 ] Features: Clones of Bartholomew Kuma (Prototypes) [ 2 ] Affiliations: Marines Occupations: Cyborgs ; human weapons Homeland: Egghead The Pacifista are human weapons ( 人間兵器 , ningen-heiki ? ) developed by the former Marine head scientist, Dr. Vegapunk . The standard models are clones of the former Warlord Bartholomew Kuma , who were cybernetically enhanced into cyborgs. [ 2 ] Kuma himself was the cyborg prototype for the project. As stated by Sentomaru , the funds required for the construction of just one Pacifista are roughly equal to that of a Marine-issued battleship. [ 3 ] Sentomaru noted that after the timeskip, the first Pacifista models were prototypes ( 試作品 , purototaipu ? ) from two years ago, meaning that the Pacifista project has been updated and modified. [ 4 ] The new models are dubbed " Mark III " ( マーク Ⅲ , Māku Surī ? , VIZ.: Mk. III) and stationed on the island of Egghead and show superiority to the outdated models. Furthermore, a small group of elite Pacifista, the Seraphim , eventually reached completion and were used in the hunt for the Seven Warlords of the Sea after the group's abolishment. [ 5 ]

name: Abdullah
affiliation: Ideo Pirates ; Straw Hat Grand Fleet
intro_text: Abdullah [ 5 ] is a criminal and a former bounty hunter who joined as a gladiator at the Corrida Colosseum to compete for the Mera Mera no Mi , along with his partner Jeet . [ 1 ] He is an ally of Luffy during the Dressrosa Arc . After the battle with the Donquixote Pirates at Dressrosa, he joined the XXX Gym Martial Arts Alliance, which later converted to a pirate crew called the Ideo Pirates . [ 2 ] [ 3 ]

[ 4 ] He was one of the Mysterious Four [ 2 ] of the Thriller Bark Pirates and one of the major antagonists of the Thriller Bark Saga . Sometime during the timeskip, he became a reporter under the alias Absa . [ 3 ] At some point, he was abducted by the Blackbeard Pirates and killed to poach his Devil Fruit , powers with his ability going to Shiryu . [ 8 ]

intro_text: "Red Flag" X Drake (pronounced as "Diez Drake") is the captain of the Marine Secret Special Unit SWORD . In order to go undercover as a pirate , he publicly defected from his position as rear admiral [ 8 ] and founded the Drake Pirates . [ 2 ] [ 1 ] He is one of twelve pirates referred to as the " Worst Generation ", [ 13 ] and before the two-year timeskip had a bounty of 222,000,000. [ 1 ] Drake is the son of the Marine-turned-pirate Diez Barrels , and was affiliated with the Barrels Pirates until he defected to the Marines. As a young adult, he was called Dory . [ 6 ] [ 14 ] In Drake's endeavor as an undercover spy, [ 2 ] he and his crew became subordinate to the Beasts Pirates , led by Kaidou of the Four Emperors , over the two-year timeskip. [ 3 ] Drake served as one of the crew's Shinuchi , specifically one of strongest six called Tobiroppo . [ 4 ] His treachery was eventually uncovered, however, leading him to ally himself with the Ninja-Pirate-Mink-Samurai Alliance . [ 5 ]

name: Ace

devil_fruit_english_name: Flame-Flame Fruit ( VIZ , Funimation ); Flare-Flare Fruit ( 4Kids , Funimation subs)
intro_text: Portgas D. Ace , [ 11 ] born as Gol D. Ace [ 6 ] and nicknamed "Fire Fist" Ace , [ 5 ] was the captain of the Spade Pirates [ 3 ] before being recruited into the Whitebeard Pirates and becoming its 2nd division commander . [ 2 ] The biological son of the late Pirate King Gol D. Roger and Portgas D. Rouge , [ 12 ] Ace was put in the care of Monkey D. Garp and Curly Dadan after Roger's death, which is where he became the sworn brother of Monkey D. Luffy [ 7 ] and Sabo . [ 13 ] Due to Roger's absence from his life, Ace disowned his father and took his mother's surname, ultimately declaring that his captain Whitebeard was his true father. [ 14 ] Ace's pursuit of his former subordinate Blackbeard for the murder of their crewmate Thatch led to him being captured after their duel on Banaro Island , and he was turned in to the Marines , who held a public execution for him on Marineford due to his heritage; this triggered the Summit War of Marineford . While freed from bondage, Ace would ultimately die at the hands of Admiral Akainu while protecting Luffy. [ 15 ] Ace's death proved the final catalyst following the Straw Hats' defeat at Sabaody that would make Luffy put his crew's journey on hiatus so they could all train for the next two years and return powerful enough to survive the New World on their own, paving the way for the second half of the series . His reincarnated Devil Fruit would eventually be consumed by his sworn brother, Sabo, ensuring that his will would live on . Ace first appeared at the end of the Drum Island Arc , but was only formally introduced in the Arabasta Arc , where he acts as a brief ally of the Straw Hat Pirates. He is a central figure during the Summit War Saga . He is also the protagonist of Ace's Great Blackbeard Search cover arc .

name: Acrobatic Fuwas
intro_text: Buggy Pirates Statistics Japanese Name: バギー海賊団 Romanized Name: Bagī Kaizokudan Official English Name: Buggy's Band of Pirates First Appearance: Chapter 8 ; Episode 4 [ 1 ] Captain: Buggy [ 2 ] ; Alvida (former, temporary during Buggy's second absence) [ 3 ] ; Richie (former, temporary during Buggy's first absence) [ 4 ] Main Ship: Big Top Total Bounty: At least 3,320,000,000 [ bounty 1 ] At least 144,000,000 [ bounty 2 ] 20,000,000 [ bounty 3 ] 15,000,000 [ bounty 4 ] The Buggy Pirates are a band of pirates led by Buggy , a former Roger Pirates apprentice . Initially an undistinguished (albeit feared) presence in the East Blue , it began pursuing a vendetta after being defeated by the Straw Hat Pirates , and followed them into the Grand Line . Since then, several unlikely circumstances and Buggy 's constantly-revolving fortunes have repeatedly overhauled it: first into an alliance with the Alvida Pirates , then into the Seven Warlords -affiliated Buggy's Delivery , [ 5 ] and finally into the Four Emperors -affiliated Cross Guild . [ 6 ] Due to their actions and role, they serve as the main antagonists of the Orange Town Arc , the protagonists of the Buggy's Crew Adventure Chronicles mini-arc, co-antagonists (alongside Smoker and his Marine unit ) in the Loguetown Arc , and side-characters in the Jaya Arc . After the timeskip , they have made tertiary appearances throughout the Dressrosa Arc , the Zou Arc , and the Wano Country Arc .

name: Adio Suerte
epithet: Master of the Gun ( 拳銃の達人 , Kenjū no Tatsujin ? )
intro_text: Adio Suerte is the last publicly known descendant of the ancient tribe who lived on the fallen sky island Waford . He is the main antagonist of One Piece Odyssey . [ 1 ]

[ 7 ] He was a Marine vice admiral , and head of G-5 , [ 3 ] who made his debut at Punk Hazard . [ 1 ] Due to his duplicitous conduct and allegiances, he can be considered the secondary antagonist of the Punk Hazard Arc . [ 2 ]

intro_text: "Flame Emperor" Sabo [ 5 ] is the Revolutionary Army 's chief of staff , recognized as the No. 2 of the entire organization under Supreme Commander Monkey D. Dragon . [ 2 ] He is also the sworn brother of the notorious pirates Monkey D. Luffy and the late Portgas D. Ace . [ 10 ] Raised in nobility by his parents Outlook III and Didit in the Goa Kingdom , Sabo ran away and met Ace and Luffy while living as a vagabond in Gray Terminal . [ 10 ] He tried to set sail just a few months afterward to get away from his family, but had his ship destroyed by the World Noble Saint Jalmack . [ 11 ] He was saved by Dragon but lost his memory in the process, and so devoted his life to the service of the Revolutionary Army. However, his memories would return 10 years later after hearing about Ace's death. [ 12 ] Two years after regaining his memories, Sabo would reunite with Luffy and also carry Ace's legacy by consuming his Devil Fruit , the Mera Mera no Mi . [ 13 ] After storming the Holy Land during the most recent Levely with several other commanders of the Revolutionary Army, Sabo was framed for the assassination of Nefertari Cobra , increasing his notoriety across the world and sparking several rebellions. [ 5 ] He acts as an ally of Luffy during the Dressrosa Arc , as well as the movies One Piece Film: Gold and One Piece: Stampede .

name: Alpha
intro_text: Alpha is a member of the CP8 branch of Cipher Pol . [ 1 ] She is the daughter of Laskey and the younger sister of CP0 agent Kalifa . [ 3 ] Posing as a nurse, she was responsible for overseeing and secretly keeping the young Jewelry Bonney as a hostage following the brokering of a deal between Bartholomew Kuma and the World Government to create the Pacifistas in exchange for performing life-saving treatment to cure Bonney's Sapphire Scales affliction. [ 1 ]

name: Alvida Pirates
intro_text: Alvida Pirates Statistics Japanese Name: アルビダ海賊団 Romanized Name: Arubida Kaizokudan Official English Name: Alvida Pirates First Appearance: Chapter 2 ; Episode 1 [ 1 ] Captain: Alvida Main Ship: Miss Love Duck Total Bounty: At least 5,000,000 [ bounty 1 ] The Alvida Pirates were a group of pirates led by the female pirate "Iron Mace" Alvida . They were the first enemy pirates Luffy was seen to encounter. The crew has since dissolved into an alliance with the Buggy Pirates , forming the Buggy and Alvida Alliance , which later becomes the Pirate Dispatch Organization known as Buggy's Delivery . They are one of the antagonist groups in the Romance Dawn Arc .

name: Amadob
intro_text: Amadob [ 4 ] is an infamous New World pirate captain who allied with Whitebeard . [ 2 ]

name: Alvida
affiliation: Cross Guild ; Buggy's Delivery , Buggy and Alvida Alliance ; Buggy Pirates (Acting Captain, temporarily); Alvida Pirates (formerly)
epithet: "Iron Mace" ( 金棒 , Kanabō ? , English versions: "Iron Club")
devil_fruit_meaning: Smooth, fat in cells, young and slippery
intro_text: "Iron Mace" Alvida [ 1 ] [ 7 ] is the former captain of the Alvida Pirates , and the first enemy pirate Monkey D. Luffy encountered in his own pirate career. [ 1 ] After her defeat by Luffy, she met and allied with Buggy to form the Buggy and Alvida Alliance , and for a time became the acting captain during Buggy's entanglements in Impel Down and the Summit War of Marineford . Since then, she has remained with the crew throughout Buggy's revolving fortunes, occupying high positions in both Buggy's Delivery [ 3 ] and the Cross Guild . [ 2 ] Due to her actions, she can be regarded as the secondary antagonist of the Romance Dawn Arc and a tertiary antagonist in the Loguetown Arc .

name: Amatsuki Toki
affiliation: Kouzuki Family ; Amatsuki Family ; Whitebeard Pirates (former); Roger Pirates (former)
intro_text: Kouzuki Toki , [ 4 ] née Amatsuki , was the wife of the daimyo of Kuri , Kouzuki Oden and the mother of Kouzuki Momonosuke and Kouzuki Hiyori . [ 5 ] She was born around 830 years before the present day. By the time she was 26 years old, she jumped forward in time with her Devil Fruit ability and arrived 30 years before the present when she met Oden [ 2 ] and stayed with him for 10 years until he was executed . She was killed during the destruction of Oden Castle . [ 1 ] [ 6 ]

name: Amazon
intro_text: Amazon [ 3 ] is an elderly resident of Skypiea who was previously the inspector of the Heaven's Gate . She let people into Skypiea even if they could not pay her extol toll, [ 1 ] but purposely failed to tell those who did not pay about the consequences for entering the city. [ 5 ] After the timeskip, she became the admission ticket saleswoman for Skypiea's new theme park, Wagomuland . [ 2 ]

name: Amazon Lily
affiliation: Kuja Kuja Pirates Monkey D. Luffy
intro_text: Amazon Lily Present Past Statistics Japanese Name: アマゾン・リリー Romanized Name: Amazon Rirī English Name: Amazon Lily First appearance: Chapter 514 ; Episode 408 Region: Calm Belt Affiliates: Kuja Kuja Pirates Monkey D. Luffy Amazon Lily is an empire located on the Island of Women ( 女ヶ島 , Nyōgashima ? , Maiden Island in the Funimation subs) , an island located in the Calm Belt adjacent to Paradise . [ 1 ] [ 2 ] The island is inhabited by the Kuja tribe. [ 1 ] Monkey D. Luffy was sent here by Bartholomew Kuma . [ 3 ] After the Empress Boa Hancock lost her status as a Seven Warlords of the Sea , Amazon Lily was invaded by the Marines and the Blackbeard Pirates and nearly destroyed by S-Hawk and Marshall D. Teach , causing the island to be deemed unsafe for the Kuja as long as Hancock remains there. [ 4 ]

name: Admiral
intro_text: Admiral Statistics Japanese Name: 大将 Romanized Name: Taishō Official English Name: Admiral First Appearance: Chapter 303 ; Episode 225 [ 1 ] Affiliation: Marines [ 1 ] Admiral is the second highest rank in the hierarchy of the Marines , senior to a Vice Admiral and junior to the Fleet Admiral . There are only three Admirals serving at a given time, and in light of their status as the strongest Marine officers, they count among the most powerful people in the World Government 's service. [ 2 ] [ 3 ] They are considered the Governors-General ( 総督 , Sōtoku ? ) of the Marine organization. [ 4 ]

name: Alpacaman
intro_text: Alpacaman is a Gifter in the Beasts Pirates who serves as a guard in the Prisoner Mines of the Udon Region of Wano Country . [ 1 ]

name: Akainu
alias: Akainu ( 赤犬 , Akainu ? , literally meaning "Red Dog")
intro_text: Sakazuki , [ 8 ] formerly known by his Admiral alias Akainu , [ 1 ] is the current Fleet Admiral of the Marines , succeeding the previous Fleet Admiral, Sengoku . [ 2 ] During the first half of the series, he was one of the three Admirals , [ 3 ] and was the last one out of the three to debut. [ 9 ] Upon becoming the Fleet Admiral, one of Sakazuki's first decisions was to relocate Marine Headquarters into the New World in preparation for the New Pirate Age . [ 10 ] In the manga, Akainu was first mentioned by Nico Robin during the initial encounter with Aokiji . [ 3 ] In the anime, he was mentioned earlier by Vice Admiral Jonathan during the anime-only G-8 Arc , which aired after Akainu had been revealed in the manga. [ 9 ] Sakazuki is perhaps the strictest follower of Absolute Justice in the entire series, to the point where his extremism prompted his fellow Admiral Aokiji to oppose his promotion to Fleet Admiral and resign from the Marines after this effort failed. [ 2 ] Due of his actions and role, Sakazuki is the main antagonist of the Marineford Arc and a major antagonist in the Final Saga and the second half of the series.

name: Ammo Knights
intro_text: Ammo Knights Statistics Japanese Name: アンモナイツ Romanized Name: Anmo Naitsu Official English Name: Ammo-Knights (VIZ) Ammo Knights (Funimation) First Appearance: Chapter 609 ; Episode 528 [ 1 ] Affiliation: Neptune Family ; Ryugu Kingdom Occupation: Guards Residency: Fish-Man Island Status: Active The Ammo Knights serve as the royal guards for the Neptune Family on Fish-Man Island . They wear large striped spiral shells as helmets and shoulder pads with their eyes covered by a black mask of some kind. The helmets have a spike sticking out in the front, similar to a that of a unicorn. They carry tridents with them. [ 1 ]

name: Anba
intro_text: Anba [ 2 ] is a man living in the Wano Country [ 1 ] and a supporter of the Kouzuki Family . [ 2 ]

name: Ange
intro_text: Ange is a giant from Elbaph . She works as a language teacher at the Walrus School , as an assistant for Jaguar D. Saul and as a librarian of the Owl Library . [ 2 ]

name: Andre
intro_text: Andre [ 3 ] is a New World pirate captain who was once a member of the Whitebeard Pirates , [ 4 ] before forming his own crew subordinate to Whitebeard . He is the twin brother of the Marine officer Candre . [ 5 ]

name: An Zengaiina
intro_text: An Zengaiina is a prisoner on Level 2 of Impel Down . [ 1 ] [ 2 ]

name: Ankoro
intro_text: Ankoro is a gigantic anglerfish formerly under the command of captain Vander Decken IX , residing in the Underworld of the Sea , seven thousand meters underneath the ocean surface. Wadatsumi seems to regard Ankoro as a pet, as apparently Ankoro was told "not to eat ships " before. [ 1 ]

name: Anjo
intro_text: Anjo is a pirate admirer who witnessed Gol D. Roger 's execution. [ 3 ]

name: Antonio
intro_text: Antonio [ 3 ] is the owner of " Antonio's Graman " in Sabaody Archipelago . [ 1 ]

name: Aphelandra
intro_text: Aphelandra is a member of the tribe of Kuja living on the island of Amazon Lily . [ 1 ] During the timeskip, she became a member of the Kuja Pirates . [ 2 ]

name: Arabasta Kingdom
english_name: Alabasta Kingdom; Arabasta (Odex); Alabaster ( One Piece: Unlimited Adventure )
intro_text: Arabasta Kingdom Statistics Japanese Name: アラバスタ王国 Romanized Name: Arabasuta Ōkoku English Name: Alabasta Kingdom; Arabasta (Odex); Alabaster ( One Piece: Unlimited Adventure ) First appearance: Chapter 113 ; Episode 78 [ 1 ] Region: Paradise Type: Summer Island [ 2 ] Population: 10,000,000 The Arabasta Kingdom is a desert kingdom in Paradise . It was the fourth place that the Straw Hat Pirates visited on the Grand Line , and the events here resulted in Luffy 's bounty being raised to 100,000,000, and Zoro acquiring a bounty of 60,000,000. It is where the poneglyph showing the location of Pluton is located, [ 3 ] and one of the twenty founding countries of the World Government . [ 4 ] It is ruled by the Nefertari Family , who, unlike the other families of the First Twenty, did not leave their home country to live in Mary Geoise .

name: Aramaki
alias: Ryokugyu ( 緑牛 , Ryokugyū ? , literally meaning "Green Bull")
intro_text: Aramaki , better known by his alias Ryokugyu , is an Admiral in the Marines . A former policeman from the Taya Kingdom , [ 3 ] he was part of the World Military Draft that filled the two Admiral vacancies (along with Fujitora ), caused by Kuzan 's resignation and Sakazuki 's promotion. [ 2 ] Ryokugyu was first mentioned by Donquixote Doflamingo in the Dressrosa Arc and debuted in the Levely Arc . His full appearance and real name were revealed near the end of the Wano Country Arc , in which he serves as a supporting antagonist. [ 6 ]

name: Arlong Pirates
intro_text: Arlong Pirates Statistics Japanese Name: アーロン一味 Romanized Name: Āron Ichimi Official English Name: Arlong's Pirates (VIZ) Arlong Pirates (Funimation) First Appearance: Chapter 69 ; Episode 31 [ 1 ] Captain: Arlong Main Ship: Shark Superb Total Bounty: At least 42,500,000 [ bounty 1 ] The Arlong Pirates were a pirate crew led by Arlong . Every member of the crew was a fish-man , except for Nami (who later left). They existed before the Sun Pirates but joined with them when they were formed. After the death of Fisher Tiger , they split from Jinbe 's crew after he became a Warlord and became their own crew once again. They believed they were above humans as the stronger species and looked down on them as inferior. After they were defeated, they were arrested. Due to their actions and role, they are the main antagonists of the Arlong Park Arc . After their leader's defeat by the Straw Hats , they are arrested and the crew itself is disbanded. Momoo was never taken into custody and Hatchan later escaped. [ 2 ]

name: Arrow
english_name: Kicking Claw Force; Elite Alabastian Royal Guards
intro_text: The Tsumegeri Guards were four of the most powerful soldiers in the Arabasta Kingdom . They drank the Hero Water and attempted to take down Crocodile , but failed and died from the water's side effects shortly after Crocodile refused to even fight them to give them an honorable death. [ 1 ]

name: Aremo Ganmi
intro_text: Aremo Ganmi [ 3 ] is a spectator at the Corrida Colosseum . She lives in Primula , a town in the west of Dressrosa . [ 3 ]

name: Arlong
affiliation: Arlong Pirates ; Sun Pirates (former); Impel Down (former)
occupation: Pirate Captain ; Prisoner of the Great Prison (former)
epithet: "Arlong the Saw" ( ノコギリのアーロン , Nokogiri no Āron ? ) (English versions: "Saw-Tooth Arlong")
intro_text: Arlong the Saw is a sawshark fish-man . He was the captain of the all fish-man crew (excluding Nami ), the Arlong Pirates , [ 1 ] a former member of the Sun Pirates , [ 2 ] and the older half-brother of Madam Shyarly . [ 3 ] He is also a major inspirational figure for the New Fish-Man Pirates . Following the death of his former captain, Fisher Tiger , Arlong was captured and sent to Impel Down , but received a pardon thanks to his comrade Jinbe becoming one of the Seven Warlords of the Sea . [ 8 ] Once freed, Arlong set his sights on conquering the East Blue , settling himself on the Conomi Islands alongside his crew and racketeering the local population. He is also the man responsible for killing Bell-mère , Nami and Nojiko 's foster mother. [ 9 ] Arlong then had Nami become his subordinate, taking advantage of her cartography skills to one day conquer all of the Blue Sea . [ 10 ] Arlong had the highest bounty in the East Blue before being defeated by Monkey D. Luffy . [ 6 ] Due to his actions and role, he is the main antagonist of the Arlong Park Arc .

name: Aruyutayan V

intro_text: Ashura Doji [ 11 ] was a bandit , samurai from Wano Country and a retainer of the Kouzuki Family , serving as one of Kouzuki Oden 's Nine Red Scabbards . [ 12 ] After Oden's death, he returned to being a criminal under the name Shutenmaru , [ 13 ] assuming leadership of the Mt. Atama Thieves . [ 2 ] He initially severed his ties with the Kouzuki Family in the present but rejoined after witnessing Yasuie 's death. [ 14 ] Ashura eventually met his demise during the Raid on Onigashima , sacrificing himself to protect his fellow Scabbards from one of Kanjuro 's schemes. [ 15 ] He is an ally of the Straw Hat Pirates during the Wano Country Arc .

intro_text: Attach , commonly nicknamed Attachan ( アタっちゃん , Atatchan ? , VIZ: "Atacchan") , is a photojournalist employed by "Big News" Morgans for the World Economy News Paper . [ 4 ] Having previously headed the Marines' Photography Department , he supplied images of many prominent outlaws—including most of the Straw Hat Pirates —for the Marines' wanted posters , [ 3 ] but was eventually dismissed for his frequent blunders. [ 5 ] Like Minatomo , Attach began as an SBS joke Eiichiro Oda used to "explain" a triviality—namely, how the Marines always had such high-quality illustrations of the world 's most dangerous and/or unreachable criminals. [ 3 ] In time, however, he became a fully canon character, with minor but tangible impact on the story.

intro_text: Kuzan , [ 6 ] [ 12 ] better known by his alias Aokiji , [ 13 ] is one of the Ten Titanic Captains of the Blackbeard Pirates , serving as the captain of the tenth ship of the fleet . [ 2 ] [ 5 ] Before joining Blackbeard 's crew, he served as a Marine Admiral , [ 1 ] having been taught by Monkey D. Garp . [ 5 ] He was nominated by Sengoku for the position of Fleet Admiral ; however, after fighting Sakazuki for the position and losing, Kuzan resigned from the Marines. [ 14 ] [ 4 ] Kuzan was the antagonist of the second part of the Long Ring Long Land Arc ; in the anime, he is instead one of the main antagonists of the Foxy's Return Arc . He is later a secondary antagonist of the Marineford Arc , a minor antagonist in the Egghead Arc , and a major antagonist in the Final Saga .

intro_text: Avalo Pizarro , [ 6 ] also known as the "Corrupt King" , is one of the Ten Titanic Captains of the Blackbeard Pirates , serving as the captain of the fourth ship of the fleet . Prior to his life of crime, he was once a king of a North Blue kingdom that eventually overthrew him for his despotic ways. [ 2 ] He was then imprisoned in Impel Down and given a life sentence in Level 6, as his crimes were so atrocious, he had to effectively be erased from history. He has since been freed by Marshall D. Teach and has become a member of his crew. [ 1 ] Due to his actions and role, he is a supporting antagonist in the Marineford Arc , a minor antagonist in the Egghead Arc , and a major antagonist in the Final Saga .

name: Bandits
intro_text: Bandits Statistics Japanese Name: 盗賊 Romanized Name: Tōzoku Official English Name: Bandits First Appearance: Chapter 1 ; Episode 4 A bandit refers to a type of organized criminal that primarily operates on land. Otherwise, they act very much like most pirates , invading and stealing from communities. Bandits in the world are regarded as the opposite to pirates in terms of views and ideals. While pirates focus on greater, legendary treasures , most bandits tend to target local villages and towns, rarely travelling to sea and remaining mostly situated in one region.

name: Baroque Works
intro_text: Baroque Works Statistics Japanese Name: バロックワークス Romanized Name: Barokku Wākusu Official English Name: Baroque Works First Appearance: Chapter 103 ; Episode 62 [ 1 ] Occupation: Underworld bounty hunters [ 2 ] President: Mr. 0 [ 3 ] Transport: Baroque Works Ships ; Banchi Coach Total Bounty: At least 389,700,000 [ bounty 1 ] Baroque Works [ 2 ] was a criminal syndicate created by the Warlord Crocodile to destabilize and conquer the Arabasta Kingdom (and, by extension, acquire the ancient superweapon Pluton ). For this sole purpose, it conducted countless acts of espionage, sabotage, and murder over a span of years, ultimately manipulating Arabasta into civil war. [ 4 ] [ 5 ] While the syndicate mostly employed common bounty hunters and mercenaries (motivated [ 3 ] by the half-truth that they were building a new, "ideal" nation), its elite members contained highly formidable warriors and spies. Despite multiple setbacks, its endgame Operation Utopia [ 6 ] nearly succeeded in every objective, and was only narrowly stopped by the combined efforts of Princess Nefertari Vivi , the Straw Hat Pirates , and a unit of Marines led by then- Captain Smoker . After Operation Utopia's failure, the syndicate was exposed and dismantled, with Crocodile and most of his subordinates—many personally defeated by the Straw Hats—arrested by the Marines. In time, however, many of them would escape and continue to impact world events . Due to their actions and role, they are collectively the main antagonists of the Arabasta Saga , with varying members being the focus of each individual arc (excluding the Drum Island Arc ). Several ex-members also serve as the protagonists of the Miss Goldenweek's "Operation: Meet Baroque Works" cover arc , and supporting characters in the Impel Down Arc and Marineford Arc .

alias: PX-0 ( PX-0 , Pīekkusu Zero ? ) "Kumachi" ( くまちー , Kuma-chī ? )
epithet: "Tyrant" ( 暴君 , Bōkun ? ) "Pacifist" ( 平和主義者 , Heiwashugi-sha ? ) "Hero" ( ヒーロー , Hīrō ? )
intro_text: Bartholomew Kuma [ 11 ] is a former Warlord of the Sea , the former king of the Sorbet Kingdom , [ 12 ] and most notably an officer , as well as founding member of the Revolutionary Army with a bounty of 296,000,000. [ 10 ] He is also the adoptive father of Jewelry Bonney , [ 13 ] daughter of his late friend and fellow revolutionary Ginny . [ 14 ] Kuma is a member of the near-extinct special race known as the Buccaneers , inherited from his late father Clapp . [ 15 ] [ 16 ] As a pirate , Kuma sailed the seas as a single-person pirate crew. [ 17 ] [ 18 ] Because of the lies told to the public by the World Government , Kuma became feared for being extremely vicious, [ 19 ] earning himself the title "Tyrant" . [ 6 ] In exchange for curing Bonney of the deadly Sapphire Scales disease , Kuma became a special type of cyborg known as a " Pacifista ", developed by the world renowned scientist Vegapunk . [ 20 ] Once Vegapunk finished his modifications on Kuma, the Warlord was converted into the first complete Pacifista cyborg, with the serial PX-0 , and mentally died in the process. [ 2 ] Vegapunk also cloned Kuma as S-Bear , one of the Seraphim that were designed to replace the Warlords. [ 21 ] Kuma has been enslaved by the World Nobles twice in his lifetime: [ 22 ] The first time was in his infancy until he escaped during the God Valley Incident [ 23 ] and the second time in adulthood as punishment for the two years he spent protecting the Thousand Sunny , [ 24 ] which lasted until his fellow revolutionaries freed him during the most recent Levely . [ 25 ] While Kuma was presented as a major antagonist during the Thriller Bark and Sabaody Archipelago Arc , he was secretly an ally. His action of separating the Straw Hat Pirates was ultimately done in their best interest, as it saved them from Admiral Kizaru and helped the crew's captain Monkey D. Luffy realize that his crew was too weak to survive in the New World and needed to train first; thus, Kuma played a key role in the subsequent timeskip . [ 26 ] In his mindless state, Kuma is a major antagonist in the Marineford Arc where he took part in the Summit War of Marineford and sided with the Marines , along with most of the other Warlords, [ 27 ] but he later becomes a major ally during the Egghead Arc and an important figure behind its ongoings.

intro_text: Bartolomeo the Cannibal [ 11 ] is a Super Rookie , the captain of the Barto Club [ 6 ] and the captain of the second ship of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet . [ 4 ] Formerly a mob boss from the East Blue , Bartolomeo was inspired to become a pirate after witnessing Monkey D. Luffy 's failed execution in Loguetown . He encountered Luffy on Dressrosa two years later while competing as a gladiator in the Corrida Colosseum , [ 12 ] and assisted the Straw Hat Pirates in overthrowing Donquixote Doflamingo . His status is currently unknown after he and his crew were sunk by the Red Hair Pirates . [ 13 ] He is an ally of Luffy during the Dressrosa Arc , the beginning of the Zou Arc , the non-canon Silver Mine Arc , and the movies One Piece: Stampede and One Piece Film: Red . He is also one of the central characters from The Stories of the Self-Proclaimed Straw Hat Grand Fleet Cover Page Serial.

intro_text: Bellamy "the Hyena" [ 3 ] [ 7 ] is a dyer and retired pirate [ 8 ] hailing from the North Blue . Originally captain of the Bellamy Pirates , [ 3 ] he was renowned in Mock Town as the "Big-Time Rookie" until his humiliating defeat by Monkey D. Luffy . [ 4 ] Desperate to redeem himself to his idol Donquixote Doflamingo , he worked back up as a full-fledged member of the Donquixote Pirates , [ 2 ] until the crew was entirely destroyed in Dressrosa by the Straw Hat Pirates . He is the main antagonist of the Jaya Arc , and a minor antagonist in the Dressrosa Arc . He is also given some focus in the The Stories of the Self-Proclaimed Straw Hat Grand Fleet cover arc .

alias: "O-Lin" ( おリン , Orin ? , English versions: "Olin")
epithet: "Big Mom" ( ビッグ・マム , Biggu Mamu ? ) (Funimation subs (formerly): Big Mam) "Evil Spirit" ( 悪神 , Akujin ? )
intro_text: Charlotte Linlin , [ 18 ] better known as "Big Mom" , [ 19 ] is the captain of the Big Mom Pirates [ 6 ] and was formerly one of the Four Emperors ruling over the New World , as the only female member of the group. [ 3 ] [ 1 ] She is the queen of the archipelago nation Totto Land , [ 6 ] a place she seeks to form into a utopia where all the world 's races can live together in peace without discrimination or segregation. [ 20 ] She is also the matriarch of the Charlotte Family , [ 5 ] whose children make up the ministers of her nation and the core infrastructure of her crew. [ 21 ] Big Mom wields the Soru Soru no Mi , which allows her to steal the souls of others and implant them in inanimate objects, resulting in the unique race of creatures known as homies , who happily serve her. Big Mom possesses three special homies: the bicorne Napoleon , the sun Prometheus , and the thunder cloud Hera . Hera's position was formerly owned by another thunder cloud, Zeus , but Big Mom grew tired of Zeus' continued failures and created Hera to replace him. [ 22 ] In her childhood, Linlin was raised on Elbaph in the Sheep's House under Carmel after being abandoned by her family. [ 23 ] After accidentally slaying Jorul , a hero of Elbaph, during one of her hunger pangs, Linlin was relocated to another island . During Linlin's birthday party, as she gorged on sweets, Carmel and her fellow orphans mysteriously disappeared and Linlin inherited Carmel's Devil Fruit. [ 24 ] Now alone, she was discovered by Streusen , and the pair co-founded a pirate crew. [ 25 ] As a young adult, Big Mom joined the legendary Rocks Pirates , where she formed a bond with apprentice Kaidou . [ 2 ] Some time after the God Valley Incident where the Rocks Pirates fell, she formed her own crew once again, the Big Mom Pirates. Big Mom developed an enmity with Monkey D. Luffy and the Straw Hat Pirates , who challenged her ownership of Fish-Man Island and then invaded Whole Cake Island, thwarting her plans to steal technology from the advanced Germa Kingdom during an arranged wedding between Sanji and her daughter, Charlotte Pudding . [ 26 ] This grudge brought her to Wano Country in pursuit of Luffy, where she formed an alliance with Kaidou and the Beasts Pirates with the intention of conquering the world and claiming the One Piece . [ 27 ] However, this never came to fruition as their alliance would go on to be defeated by the Ninja-Pirate-Mink-Samurai Alliance that same night. [ 28 ] Following her defeat to Eustass Kid and Trafalgar Law , the World Economy News Paper released a new listing of the Four Emperors, with Big Mom no longer being included among them anymore. [ 29 ] As a young child at the beginning of her piracy career, Big Mom was assigned a 50,000,000 bounty which was quickly escalated to 500,000,000. [ 12 ] By the time of the Raid on Onigashima , at the peak of her piracy career, she held a bounty of 4,388,000,000 which was the second-highest bounty of any living pirate . [ 11 ] Big Mom was the last of the Four Emperors to be named; her epithet was first revealed after the Summit War of Marineford by Eustass Kid [ 4 ] and her real name was revealed by Pappag on Fish-Man Island. [ 19 ] Since the timeskip , she is a minor antagonist during the Fish-Man Island Arc , the main antagonist of the Whole Cake Island Arc and the Whole Cake Island Saga as a whole, the secondary antagonist of the second half of the Wano Country Arc and one of the two secondary antagonists of Wano Country Saga as a whole.

name: Big Pan
epithet: Big Pan "the Wotan" ( ウォータンのビッグパン , Wōtan no Biggu Pan ? )
intro_text: Big Pan is the largest former member of the Foxy Pirates and a combatant of the Groggy Monsters. [ 1 ] He is a wotan , being half loach fish-man and half giant . [ 3 ] Due to his actions, he is a major antagonist during the Long Ring Long Land Arc .

name: Bentham‎
occupation: Queen of Newkama Land ; Officer Agent (former); Prisoner of the Great Prison
alias: "Mr. 2 Bon Kurei" ( Mr. 2 ・ボン・クレー , Misutā Tsū Bon Kurē ? , Official Translation: Mr. 2 Bon Clay)
epithet: "Bentham of the Wild" ( 荒野のベンサム , Kōya no Bensamu ? )
intro_text: Bentham of the Wild , alias Mr. 2 Bon Kurei , [ 7 ] [ 4 ] is a newkama and former Baroque Works officer agent . [ 8 ] [ 3 ] He was a major antagonist during the Arabasta Arc , and (along with his fellow Officer Agents) one of the central characters in Miss Goldenweek's "Operation: Meet Baroque Works" . While he was once an enemy of Luffy 's as a member of Baroque Works, [ 9 ] the two became quick friends, leading Bon Kurei to sacrifice himself for Luffy both at Arabasta [ 10 ] and later at Impel Down [ 11 ] , being one of his main allies during that Story Arc . He is currently the queen of Newkama Land . [ 2 ]

name: Beasts Pirates
english_name: Animal Kingdom Pirates (VIZ Media and Funimation sub); Beast Pirates (Funimation dub, Funimation sub (former))
intro_text: Beasts Pirates Statistics Japanese Name: 百獣海賊団 Romanized Name: Hyakujū Kaizokudan Official English Name: Animal Kingdom Pirates (VIZ Media and Funimation sub); Beast Pirates (Funimation dub, Funimation sub (former)) First Appearance: Chapter 595 ; Episode 513 [ 1 ] Governor-General: Kaidou Total Bounty: At least 11,179,100,000 [ bounty 1 ] At least 11,401,100,000 [ bounty 2 ] The Beasts Pirates [ 2 ] were an extremely infamous and powerful pirate crew led by their governor-general , Kaidou , formerly one of the Four Emperors . They were based in Wano Country , specifically headquartered on Onigashima . [ 3 ] [ 4 ] For the past 26 years, the Beasts Pirates consolidated their power in Wano, which they occupied through an alliance with the country's shogun , Kurozumi Orochi . [ 3 ] [ 5 ] Within the past three years, the crew partnered with Donquixote Doflamingo and Caesar Clown to create artificial Zoan Devil Fruits called SMILE Fruits, leading to the crew having over 500 users with Devil Fruit powers. After the Ninja-Pirate-Mink-Samurai Alliance captured Caesar, destroyed the SMILE Factory and defeated Doflamingo, Kaidou turned to a new alliance with the Big Mom Pirates . The two Emperors' alliance planned to conquer the world by obtaining the Ancient Weapons and the One Piece . [ 6 ] Part of this plan is Kaidou's " New Onigashima Project ", which sought to replace Orochi as shogun with Yamato during the latest Fire Festival . [ 7 ] This scheme ultimately failed, as during the Raid on Onigashima , all their top officers, alongside his fellow Emperor Big Mom , were defeated one after another by the Ninja-Pirate-Mink-Samurai Alliance and Kaidou himself was defeated by Monkey D. Luffy , captain of the Straw Hat Pirates . [ 8 ] After the battle, Kaidou was sent flying down into an underground magma chamber and was caught in a massive volcanic explosion, with his fate remaining unclear, although it is largely shown to be defunct. [ 9 ] Kaidou is no longer considered one of the Four Emperors following the announcement of his defeat, leaving the overall status of the Beasts Pirates unknown. The vast majority of the remaining members were eventually captured by the Marines , while a minority who defected to the Ninja-Pirate-Mink-Samurai Alliance became citizens of Wano. Due to their actions and role, they are the main antagonists of the Zou Arc and Wano Country Arc , and one of the central antagonist groups of the Whole Cake Island Saga and Wano Country Saga .

name: Bingo
intro_text: Bingo is a former priest and cooper who currently lives in Ebisu Town in Wano Country . He formerly lived in the Flower Capital before losing all his money and being exiled. [ 3 ]

name: Bimine
intro_text: Bimine [ 1 ] is the mayor of Pucci , "The Gourmet Town". He was first mentioned when the town of Pucci was brought up by Kalifa . [ 2 ]

name: Billions
intro_text: Baroque Works Statistics Japanese Name: バロックワークス Romanized Name: Barokku Wākusu Official English Name: Baroque Works First Appearance: Chapter 103 ; Episode 62 [ 1 ] Occupation: Underworld bounty hunters [ 2 ] President: Mr. 0 [ 3 ] Transport: Baroque Works Ships ; Banchi Coach Total Bounty: At least 389,700,000 [ bounty 1 ] Baroque Works [ 2 ] was a criminal syndicate created by the Warlord Crocodile to destabilize and conquer the Arabasta Kingdom (and, by extension, acquire the ancient superweapon Pluton ). For this sole purpose, it conducted countless acts of espionage, sabotage, and murder over a span of years, ultimately manipulating Arabasta into civil war. [ 4 ] [ 5 ] While the syndicate mostly employed common bounty hunters and mercenaries (motivated [ 3 ] by the half-truth that they were building a new, "ideal" nation), its elite members contained highly formidable warriors and spies. Despite multiple setbacks, its endgame Operation Utopia [ 6 ] nearly succeeded in every objective, and was only narrowly stopped by the combined efforts of Princess Nefertari Vivi , the Straw Hat Pirates , and a unit of Marines led by then- Captain Smoker . After Operation Utopia's failure, the syndicate was exposed and dismantled, with Crocodile and most of his subordinates—many personally defeated by the Straw Hats—arrested by the Marines. In time, however, many of them would escape and continue to impact world events . Due to their actions and role, they are collectively the main antagonists of the Arabasta Saga , with varying members being the focus of each individual arc (excluding the Drum Island Arc ). Several ex-members also serve as the protagonists of the Miss Goldenweek's "Operation: Meet Baroque Works" cover arc , and supporting characters in the Impel Down Arc and Marineford Arc .

intro_text: Blackbeard Pirates Statistics Japanese Name: 黒ひげ海賊団 Romanized Name: Kurohige Kaizokudan Official English Name: Black Beard Pirates (Edited dub); Blackbeard Pirates (Uncut dub) First Appearance: Chapter 234 ; Episode 151 [ 1 ] Admiral: Marshall D. Teach [ 1 ] Main Ship: Saber of Xebec [ 2 ] Total Bounty: Main Crew: At least 4,194,200,000 [ bounty 1 ] [ 3 ] At least 2,445,800,000 [ bounty 2 ] [ 4 ] At least 198,200,000 [ bounty 3 ] [ 5 ] Organization: At least 4,246,200,000 [ bounty 4 ] The Blackbeard Pirates [ 6 ] are an extremely infamous and powerful pirate fleet led by their admiral , Marshall D. Teach , better known as "Blackbeard" , one of the Four Emperors . [ 1 ] Blackbeard was originally a member of the Whitebeard Pirates (and Portgas D. Ace 's subordinate), until he murdered one of his crewmates [ 7 ] over a Devil Fruit and defected, [ 8 ] heading out to form his own crew. Blackbeard was Crocodile 's replacement among the Seven Warlords of the Sea , having captured the wanted pirate Ace to obtain his position, but resigned after forfeiting the privilege by raiding Impel Down [ 9 ] and recruiting several notorious prisoners for his crew from Level 6. [ 10 ] During the timeskip, after clashing with the remnants of the Whitebeard Pirates led by Marco the Phoenix and emerging victorious, [ 11 ] Blackbeard usurped the status of the deceased Whitebeard as one of the Four Emperors. [ 12 ] Following the Rocky Port Incident , the Blackbeard Pirates made Hachinosu their main base. [ 13 ] Before the timeskip , they served as unseen antagonists in the Drum Island Arc , minor antagonists in the Jaya Arc and Post-Enies Lobby Arc , and the overarching antagonist group in the Impel Down Arc , Marineford Arc , and Summit War Saga as a whole. After the timeskip , they served as minor antagonists during the Dressrosa Arc and Wano Country Arc , and a major antagonist group during the Egghead Arc and the Final Saga as a whole.

name: Big Mom Pirates
intro_text: Big Mom Pirates Statistics Japanese Name: ビッグ・マム海賊団 Romanized Name: Biggu Mamu Kaizokudan Official English Name: Big Mom Pirates First Appearance: Chapter 651 ; Episode 570 [ 1 ] Captain: Charlotte Linlin Main Ship: Queen Mama Chanter Total Bounty: Main Crew: At least 10,121,500,000 [ bounty 1 ] 500,000,000 [ bounty 2 ] 50,000,000 [ bounty 3 ] Organization: At least 10,121,500,000 [ bounty 4 ] At least 11,044,500,000 [ bounty 5 ] The Big Mom Pirates [ 1 ] are an extremely infamous and powerful pirate crew led by their captain , Charlotte Linlin , better known as "Big Mom" , formerly one of the Four Emperors . They are in control of the powerful nation of Totto Land , with their captain ruling it as its queen and their base of operations being Whole Cake Island , the country's main island. The captain agreed to an alliance with the Beasts Pirates for plans to take over the world . [ 2 ] However, the captain was defeated during the Raid on Onigashima by Eustass Kid and Trafalgar Law of the Worst Generation , [ 3 ] and she was subsequently stripped of her position as an Emperor, leaving the crew's status unknown. Due to their actions and role, they are minor antagonists during the Fish-Man Island Arc , Dressrosa Arc , and Zou Arc , and the main antagonists of the Whole Cake Island Arc and Whole Cake Island Saga as a whole.

name: Blackbeard
japanese_name: マーシャル・ D ・ティーチ
english_name: Marshall D. Teech (VIZ, Funimation subs, edited dub); Marshall D. Teach (Funimation uncut dub)
affiliation: Blackbeard Pirates ; Four Emperors ; Clan of D. ( Davy Family ); Seven Warlords of the Sea (former); Whitebeard Pirates 2nd Division (defected)
occupation: Pirate ; Admiral ; Emperor ; Apprentice (former); Warlord of the Sea (former)
epithet: "Blackbeard" ( 黒ひげ , Kurohige ? ) ("Black Beard" in the edited dub)
intro_text: Marshall D. Teach , [ 19 ] most commonly referred to by his epithet "Blackbeard" , [ 2 ] is the admiral of the Blackbeard Pirates , [ 9 ] and one of the Four Emperors that rule over the New World . [ 3 ] He is the son of the legendary pirate Rocks D. Xebec [ 20 ] and his wife Eris , making him a member of the nearly-extinct Davy Family and a descendant of Davy D. Jones , [ 21 ] which is only known to a small number of individuals, including his own crew, the Five Elders , [ 22 ] and Nerona Imu . [ 23 ] He is the only known person in history to wield the powers of two Devil Fruits . [ 24 ] Teach started out his pirate career as an apprentice of the Whitebeard Pirates , then later became a member of the crew's 2nd Division. [ 7 ] However, he murdered Thatch , the 4th Division Commander, in order to steal the Yami Yami no Mi , a rare darkness-based Logia Devil Fruit with the power to nullify the Devil Fruit abilities of others. [ 8 ] He then fled from Whitebeard's crew and, after defeating the infamous 2nd Division Commander Portgas D. Ace and handing him over to the Marines , [ 5 ] was awarded a position among the Seven Warlords of the Sea . He only held the position briefly, though, using it to infiltrate the great underwater prison Impel Down and escaping with multiple infamous Level 6 prisoners recruited into his crew. Upon arriving at the Summit War of Marineford , [ 6 ] he resigned from his position and murdered Whitebeard , mysteriously stealing his quake-generating power . This, along with his victory in the Payback War [ 25 ] and conquest of the majority of Whitebeard's territories during the timeskip , resulted in his acknowledgement as one of the Four Emperors, as well as him being considered a part of the " Worst Generation " along with the eleven Supernovas . [ 26 ] After the Rocky Port Incident , he turned the island of Hachinosu into his base of operations with the ambition of having it officially accepted into the World Government as the Blackbeard Kingdom. [ 27 ] [ 28 ] Teach initially had no bounty ; [ 5 ] however, after resigning from the Seven Warlords, recruiting several dangerous criminals to his crew from Impel Down, and becoming one of the Four Emperors, he received a bounty of 2,247,600,000. [ 18 ] Following the downfalls of Kaidou and Big Mom during the Wano Country Arc , his bounty was increased to 3,996,000,000 for unknown reasons. [ 17 ] Due to his actions throughout the series, namely his current plans and his key role in the deaths of Portgas D. Ace and Whitebeard , Teach serves as a central antagonist of the entire series. During the series' first half , he is an unseen minor antagonist in the Drum Island Arc , a minor antagonist in the Jaya Arc , the secondary antagonist of the Post-Enies Lobby Arc , and the overarching antagonist of the Summit War Saga . During the series' second half , he is a minor antagonist in the Dressrosa Arc and Wano Country Arc , a supporting antagonist in the Egghead Arc , and one of the main antagonists of the Final Saga .

name: Boa Hancock
affiliation: Kuja Pirates ( Gorgon Sisters ); Seven Warlords of the Sea (former)
occupation: Captain of the Kuja Pirates; Empress of Amazon Lily ; Warlord of the Sea (former); Slave (former)
epithet: "Snake Princess" ( 蛇姫 , Hebihime ? ) ; "Pirate Empress" ( 海賊女帝 , Kaizoku Jotei ? )
intro_text: "Pirate Empress" Boa Hancock [ 10 ] is the current captain of the Kuja Pirates and was the only female Warlord of the Sea before the organization's disbandment. She is the current empress of Amazon Lily , [ 4 ] also known as the "Snake Princess" . As the current empress she succeeds former empresses Tritoma , Shakuyaku , and Gloriosa . She rules over the Kuja tribe with her two younger sisters, Boa Sandersonia and Boa Marigold . [ 4 ] Kidnapped by the World Nobles as a child along with her sisters, she was enslaved and tortured for years; during this time she was force-fed the Mero Mero no Mi , which gives her the ability to turn both living and non-living things into stone. She and her sisters escaped from Mary Geoise during a mass breakout instigated by Fisher Tiger . She was returned to her home with the help of Gloriosa, Silvers Rayleigh and Shakuyaku, where she would eventually become the island's empress, as well as one of the Seven Warlords. Though initially antagonistic towards Monkey D. Luffy , his selfless actions and animosity towards the Celestial Dragons caused her to befriend, and eventually fall in love, with him. She went on to serve as one of Luffy's most stalwart allies, helping him infiltrate Impel Down , siding with him in the Summit War of Marineford , providing him a location to train on for two years, and then helping him reunite with his crew and supporting him from afar. While employed within the Seven Warlords, she turned against the World Government on a number of occasions to support Luffy, though she was able to retain her title by feigning innocence. However, the Warlords were officially abolished after the most recent Levely , causing her to lose her status and became one of the world's most wanted criminals. [ 11 ] Vegapunk was able to clone Hancock as one of the Seraphim , S-Snake , to replace the Warlords. [ 12 ] She first gained a bounty of 80,000,000 due to her being a captain of the Kuja Pirates before it was frozen after she joined the Seven Warlords. [ 9 ] However, after the abolition of the Seven Warlords, Hancock's bounty was reinstated and raised to 1,659,000,000. [ 8 ] She initially serves as the main antagonist of the Amazon Lily Arc , before becoming a major ally for the rest of the Summit War Saga , as well as the Return to Sabaody Arc . She also acts as an ally of Luffy during the 3D 2Y special, the Cidre Guild filler Arc , and the movie One Piece: Stampede .

intro_text: Boo is a member of the Chinjao Family and the vice-leader of the Happo Navy . Additionally, he acted as an ally of Luffy during the Dressrosa Arc .

[ 1 ] All of the ducks have the strange theme of wearing different types of hats and various other jewelry and clothing. Also strange was that the last member to join the squad, Matsuge , is a camel who helped the Straw Hats confront Baroque Works .

intro_text: Borsalino , [ 8 ] [ 9 ] more commonly known by his alias Kizaru , [ 1 ] is an Admiral in the Marines . [ 2 ] After Kuzan resigned from the Marines and Sakazuki was promoted to Fleet Admiral, Kizaru is now the only remaining Admiral from the previous trio. He was first mentioned by Nico Robin during the Straw Hats' initial encounter with Aokiji. [ 2 ] Due to his actions and role, Kizaru is the secondary antagonist of the Sabaody Archipelago Arc and Marineford Arc , and the tertiary antagonist of the Egghead Arc .

name: Buffalo
intro_text: Buffalo is a combatant and an assassin of the Donquixote Pirates [ 1 ] and an officer of the Pica Army. [ 5 ] His real name is yet to be revealed, as "Buffalo" is a codename given to him upon joining the Donquixote Pirates. [ 6 ] Due to his actions and role, he is a minor antagonist during the Punk Hazard Arc , and later a supporting antagonist during the Dressrosa Arc .

name: Buckingham Stussy
japanese_name: ミス・バッキンガム・ステューシー
occupation: Pirate (former); Scientist (former, self-proclaimed); Science Robber ( 科学強盗 , Kagaku Gōtō ? )
alias: Miss Buckin ( ミス・バッキン , Misu Bakkin ? )
intro_text: Miss Buckingham Stussy, also known by her alias Miss Buckin , is a former member of both the Rocks Pirates [ 2 ] and the illegal science institute MADS . [ 3 ] She is the self-proclaimed lover of the deceased Emperor Edward Newgate and the mother of Edward Weevil , a former Warlord of the Sea . [ 1 ] During her time with MADS, she worked alongside other notable scientists like Dr. Vegapunk , Caesar Clown , Queen , and Vinsmoke Judge , though it has been stated that she was a freeloader who simply took and benefited from their research, hardly contributing anything of her own. Nonetheless, the group did manage to successfully produce a clone of her, Stussy , who is under the employment of Vegapunk. [ 2 ]

name: Buggy Pirates
intro_text: Buggy Pirates Statistics Japanese Name: バギー海賊団 Romanized Name: Bagī Kaizokudan Official English Name: Buggy's Band of Pirates First Appearance: Chapter 8 ; Episode 4 [ 1 ] Captain: Buggy [ 2 ] ; Alvida (former, temporary during Buggy's second absence) [ 3 ] ; Richie (former, temporary during Buggy's first absence) [ 4 ] Main Ship: Big Top Total Bounty: At least 3,320,000,000 [ bounty 1 ] At least 144,000,000 [ bounty 2 ] 20,000,000 [ bounty 3 ] 15,000,000 [ bounty 4 ] The Buggy Pirates are a band of pirates led by Buggy , a former Roger Pirates apprentice . Initially an undistinguished (albeit feared) presence in the East Blue , it began pursuing a vendetta after being defeated by the Straw Hat Pirates , and followed them into the Grand Line . Since then, several unlikely circumstances and Buggy 's constantly-revolving fortunes have repeatedly overhauled it: first into an alliance with the Alvida Pirates , then into the Seven Warlords -affiliated Buggy's Delivery , [ 5 ] and finally into the Four Emperors -affiliated Cross Guild . [ 6 ] Due to their actions and role, they serve as the main antagonists of the Orange Town Arc , the protagonists of the Buggy's Crew Adventure Chronicles mini-arc, co-antagonists (alongside Smoker and his Marine unit ) in the Loguetown Arc , and side-characters in the Jaya Arc . After the timeskip , they have made tertiary appearances throughout the Dressrosa Arc , the Zou Arc , and the Wano Country Arc .

name: Buggy's Delivery
japanese_name: バギーズ デリバリー
intro_text: Buggy's Delivery Statistics Japanese Name: バギーズ デリバリー Romanized Name: Bagīzu Deribarī Official English Name: Buggy's Delivery First Appearance: Chapter 706 ; Episode 639 [ 1 ] Affiliation: Seven Warlords of the Sea Occupation: Mercenaries President: Buggy Total Bounty: Organization: At least 44,000,000 [ bounty 1 ] Buggy's Delivery , also known as the Pirate Dispatch Organization ( 海賊派遣組織 , Kaizoku Haken Soshiki ? , VIZ: "Pirate Temp Agency") , [ 2 ] was a pirate mercenary group founded by Buggy the Star Clown . [ 3 ] The organization has since been integrated into the Cross Guild . [ 4 ]

name: Buhichuck
intro_text: Buhichuck was the boss of the " Surprise Zombies " in the dining room.

name: Buggy and Alvida Alliance
intro_text: Buggy and Alvida Alliance Statistics Japanese Name: バギー&アルビダ連合 Romanized Name: Bagī ando Arubida Rengō Official English Name: Buggy and Alvida Alliance First Appearance: Chapter 54 ; Episode 46 Captain: Buggy ; Alvida Main Ship: Big Top Total Bounty: Formerly more than 44,000,000 The Buggy and Alvida Alliance [ 1 ] was an alliance formed by the pirate bands known as the Buggy Pirates and the Alvida Pirates (although only its captain remained from them) and led by the captains Buggy and Alvida . Although both crews were the first opponents to be defeated by the Straw Hat Pirates , the alliance remained relevant in the story. Its last known goal was finding Captain John 's legendary treasure. The alliance eventually evolved into the Pirate Dispatch Organization, Buggy's Delivery , after Buggy gained new fame and followers as a Warlord . [ 2 ] After Buggy lost his Warlord status, the alliance became integrated into the Cross Guild , which Buggy was deemed the leader of and labeled a member of the Four Emperors as a result.

name: Buggy
affiliation: Cross Guild ; Buggy's Delivery (Unknown status) ? ; Buggy Pirates ; Buggy and Alvida Alliance ; Four Emperors ; Seven Warlords of the Sea (former); Roger Pirates (former); Impel Down (former)
occupation: Pirate Captain ; Emperor ; Figurehead President of Cross Guild; Chairman of Buggy's Delivery (former); Warlord of the Sea (former); Apprentice (former); Prisoner of the Great Prison (former)
epithet: "Buggy the Clown" ( 道化のバギー , Dōke no Bagī ? ) "Buggy the Star Clown" ( 千両道化のバギー , Senryō Dōke no Bagī ? , English versions: Buggy the Genius Jester)
intro_text: Buggy the Star Clown is the nominal president of Cross Guild , and one of the current Four Emperors dominating the New World . [ 6 ] [ 2 ] [ 16 ] Initially a Roger Pirates apprentice alongside Shanks , [ 8 ] Buggy succeeded his disbanded mentors as a career pirate in the East Blue , in time becoming captain of the Buggy Pirates . One of the earliest enemies defeated by Monkey D. Luffy and the Straw Hat Pirates , [ 4 ] revenge drove him to an alliance with "Iron Mace" Alvida and pursuit into the Grand Line . [ 5 ] Since then, his fortunes have constantly, dramatically revolved: from imprisonment in Impel Down to entanglement in its first mass-breakout and the subsequent Summit War of Marineford , his escalating infamy won him entry to the Seven Warlords of the Sea , [ 7 ] allowing him to launch the pirate mercenary organization Buggy's Delivery . [ 3 ] After the Warlords were outlawed, [ 17 ] Buggy found himself "leading" the Cross Guild, a successor group actually controlled by fellow ex-Warlords Crocodile and Dracule Mihawk ; with his inflated reputation, he was grudgingly retained as figurehead leader, and gained recognition as one of the new Emperors. [ 16 ] Emboldened, Buggy has declared his intent to become the Pirate King, putting Cross Guild in pursuit of One Piece . [ 18 ] He is the main antagonist of the Orange Town Arc and a secondary antagonist in the Loguetown Arc , as well as the central protagonist of the Buggy's Crew Adventure Chronicles cover arc (the manga's first). Since then, he has become one of the series' most-recurring characters, serving as a supporting character through the Summit War Saga in addition to tertiary appearances in the Jaya Arc , Zou Arc , Wano Country Arc , and Egghead Arc . He is also the main antagonist in most episodes of " Boss Luffy Historical Special ". He also serves as a reluctant ally to Luffy in the 3D 2Y special episode, and the movie One Piece: Stampede .

name: Brogy
epithet: Brogy the Red Ogre ( 赤鬼のブロギー , Aka-Oni no Burogī ? )
intro_text: Brogy the Red Ogre is a giant from Elbaph and one of the two captains of the Giant Warrior Pirates alongside Dorry . Once an active and infamous threat in the seas long ago, Brogy became embroiled in a personal duel over a trivial dispute with his best friend and co-captain Dorry. This duel on Little Garden lasted for a century before being interrupted by the arrival of the Straw Hat Pirates and the machinations of Baroque Works . After finally settling their duel the two giant captains returned to Elbaph and their crew, and later became involved in the Egghead Incident to rescue the Straw Hat Pirates and bring them to safety to Elbaph. A century ago his bounty was 100,000,000, but due to years of inflation and a return to active piracy his bounty was updated to 1,800,000,000. He is a major ally of the Straw Hat Pirates during the Little Garden , Egghead , and Elbaph Arcs. After being transformed into a demon by Imu , he temporarily served as a minor antagonist during the Elbaph Arc.

name: Brook
affiliation: Straw Hat Pirates ; Straw Hat Grand Fleet ; Rumbar Pirates (former); Ninja-Pirate-Mink-Samurai Alliance (disbanded)
occupation: Musician ; Swordsman ; Senior Officer ; Pirate Captain (acting, former); Battle Convoy leader (former); World-Famous Rock Star (former)
alias: "Demon King Satan-sama" ( 悪魔王サタン様 , Akuma-ō Satan-sama ? , [EN] [-] VIZ: "Great Demon Lord"; Funimation subs: "King of the Devils - Satan-sama"; Funimation dub: "Lord Satan" ) ; "Hone-kichi" ( ホネ 吉 , Honekichi ? , [EN] [-] English versions: Bonekichi ) ; "The Starving Skeleton" ( がしゃどくろ , Gashadokuro ? )
epithet: "Humming Brook" ( 鼻歌のブルック , Hanauta no Burukku ? ) ; "Soul King" ( ソウルキング , Souru Kingu ? )
intro_text: "Soul King" Brook [ 11 ] is the musician of the Straw Hat Pirates , one of their two swordsmen , and one of the Senior Officers of the Straw Hat Grand Fleet . [ 3 ] He is the ninth member of the crew and the eighth to join, [ 17 ] doing so at the end of the Thriller Bark Arc . Brook ate the Yomi Yomi no Mi , which allowed him to return to life after death once. [ 18 ] Brook eventually learned to tap deeper into the powers of his Devil Fruit, giving him significant control over his own soul and the souls of others. Originally a member of the Rumbar Pirates , he died and was resurrected through the power of the Yomi Yomi no Mi. However, due to the amount of time it took for his soul to find his body, it was reduced to a skeleton, keeping only his afro intact. [ 18 ] Brook drifted alone in the Florian Triangle for 50 years, eventually meeting Luffy and serving as the Straw Hats' ally during the Thriller Bark Arc before officially joining the crew. His dream is to reunite with his old friend, Laboon , at Reverse Mountain , where he resides with Crocus . [ 19 ] He first gained a bounty of 33,000,000 during his time as a member of the Rumbar Pirates. It later increased to 83,000,000 after the Dressrosa Arc . Following the Raid on Onigashima , his bounty was increased to 383,000,000. [ 3 ]

name: Bulldog
intro_text: Bulldog is the son of Conney and the king of the Sorbet Kingdom . He held the throne one generation before Bekori ’s reign, and reclaimed his position after Bekori's successor Bartholomew Kuma became a fugitive. [ 1 ]

intro_text: Bungo is an arsonist in Wano Country who currently lives in Ebisu Town . [ 2 ] He formerly lived in the Flower Capital , but was exiled after being swindled by Komurasaki . [ 1 ]

devil_fruit_english_name: Cat-Cat Fruit Leopard Model (VIZ); Cat-Cat Fruit, Model: Leopard (VIZ, Funimation)
intro_text: Rob Lucci , [ 1 ] [ 13 ] also known as "Massacre Weapon" , [ 5 ] is a masked agent of CP0 . [ 3 ] Originally, he was introduced as part of CP9 , reputed as the assassin unit's strongest member in history. [ 14 ] Operating undercover to obtain the Pluton blueprints from Iceburg , [ 4 ] he worked as a sawyer, treenail, and bolt specialist for the Galley-La Company 's Dock 1 in Water 7 . [ 1 ] After his defeat by Monkey D. Luffy at Enies Lobby , Lucci was dismissed from CP9 by Spandam , who declared him and the other assassins responsible for the Straw Hats ' havoc on Enies Lobby. However, within the next two years, he managed to be reinstated into Cipher Pol as part of CP0, this time as Spandam's superior. [ 3 ] Lucci is the secondary antagonist of the Water 7 Saga , appearing as the main antagonist of the Water 7 Arc and the secondary antagonist of the Enies Lobby Arc . He later appears as a major antagonist in the Egghead Arc (and briefly a reluctant ally). He is also one of the central characters from CP9's Independent Report Cover Page Serial, along with the rest of the CP9 agents.

name: Bushon
intro_text: Bushon [ 1 ] is one of the Sea Train organizers in Water 7 , along with Stevie .

name: CB Gallant
intro_text: CB Gallant [ 3 ] was a member of the Roger Pirates until the crew's dissolution. [ 1 ] His current whereabouts and status are unknown.

name: Byron
intro_text: Byron is one of the slaves who was sold at the Human Auctioning House and was the first in the queue for sale. He is a pirate and musician . [ 1 ]

name: Bunny Joe

name: CP0
intro_text: CP0 Statistics Japanese Name: CP0 Romanized Name: Shī-Pī-Zero Official English Name: CP-0 First Appearance: Chapter 705 ; Episode 635 [ 1 ] Chief: Spandam (former) [ 2 ] CP0 , more properly known as Cipher Pol "Aigis" Zero ( サイファーポール"イージス"ゼロ , Saifā Pōru Ījisu Zero ? ) , is the strongest intelligence agency among Cipher Pol , working directly under the World Nobles . They first appeared on the northeast of Dressrosa near Cafe Bar La Baltad. [ 1 ] Due to their actions and role, they are major antagonists during the Levely Arc , the secondary antagonist group of the Wano Country Arc and Egghead Arc , and one of the central antagonist groups throughout the second half of the series.

name: CP5
intro_text: CP5 Statistics Japanese Name: CP5 Romanized Name: Shī Pī Faibu Official English Name: CP5 First Appearance: Chapter 355 ; Episode 249 [ 1 ] Affiliation: World Government Occupation: Cipher Pol [ 1 ] Leader(s): Unknown; Spandam (formerly) [ 1 ] CP5 , more formally known as Cipher Pol Number 5 ( サイファーポールナンバーファイブ , Saifā Pōru Nanbā Faibu ? ) is an Intelligence Agency, working under the World Government , being one of the Cipher Pols . It was led by Spandam . [ 1 ]

name: CP9
intro_text: CP9 Statistics Japanese Name: CP9 Romanized Name: Shī-Pī-Nain Official English Name: CP9 First Appearance: Chapter 325 ; Episode 230 Affiliation: World Government [ 1 ] Occupation: World Government Secret Assassins [ 1 ] Chief: Spandine (former); Spandam (former) Transport: Puffing Tom CP9 , more properly known as Cipher Pol Number 9 ( サイファーポールNo.9 , Saifā Pōru Nanbā Nain ? ) , is a secret Cipher Pol agency, [ 1 ] formed of people able to use the Six Powers techniques. [ 2 ] They are the main antagonist group of the Water 7 Saga , specifically the Water 7 and Enies Lobby Arcs. The organization's agents are also the central characters from CP9's Independent Report Cover Page Serial.

Due to his actions, he can be considered the tertiary antagonist of the Orange Town Arc , and a minor antagonist in the Loguetown Arc .

name: Camel

intro_text: Camie [ 3 ] is a kissing gourami mermaid that first appeared in Hatchan's Sea-Floor Stroll cover story. She first appeared in the main series after being spat out of a large sea boar that was saved by Hatchan , along with her starfish pet and master, Pappag . [ 5 ] While dreaming of becoming a designer, [ 6 ] she worked on Hatchan's Takoyaki 8 when she first met the Straw Hat Pirates . After the timeskip, she is a waitress at the Mermaid Cafe on Fish-Man Island . [ 7 ] She is a central character during the Sabaody Archipelago Arc and also an ally of the Straw Hats in the Fish-Man Island Arc .

name: Candre
intro_text: Candre is Andre 's twin brother and an officer within the Marines . [ 5 ]

name: Captain
intro_text: Captain Statistics Japanese Name: 大佐 Romanized Name: Taisa Official English Name: Captain First Appearance: Chapter 4 ; Episode 2 [ 1 ] Affiliation: Marines Captain is the sixth highest rank in the Marines . [ 2 ] The rank of a marine officer who commands a ship would not necessarily be "Captain". It may be higher (up to Fleet Admiral ), or lower on small ships. By naval custom, any officer who is below the rank of Captain and commands a ship is addressed as "captain" while aboard that ship. The ship's captain also may be referred to as the Skipper.

name: Capote
intro_text: Capote is a billfish fish-man and a former combatant of the Foxy Pirates . Due to his actions, he is a major antagonist during the Long Ring Long Land Arc .

name: Capone Bege
affiliation: Fire Tank Pirates ; Big Mom Pirates (former)
epithet: "Gang" ( ギャング , Gyangu ? )
intro_text: Capone "Gang" Bege [ 10 ] is a mafia don -turned- pirate and the captain of the Fire Tank Pirates . He is also one of twelve pirates who are referred to as the " Worst Generation ". [ 11 ] He and his crew came from West Blue , where Bege was one of the heads of the Five Families of the West . [ 3 ] During the timeskip, he became a subordinate of Charlotte Linlin [ 2 ] and married Charlotte Chiffon . The two have a son named Capone Pez . [ 12 ] While working under the Big Mom Pirates , he held a position of a rook-ranked combatant . [ 13 ] Due to his allegiance and actions, he is the secondary antagonist of the Zou Arc . Later, Bege formed a temporary alliance with the Straw Hat Pirates in order to kill Big Mom during the Whole Cake Island Arc . [ 14 ] After his plan failed, Bege effectively became an enemy of the Emperor and her crew. Sometime during the timeskip, his bounty increased from 138,000,000 to 300,000,000. [ 8 ] After the events of the Whole Cake Island Arc , it has increased to 350,000,000. [ 7 ] He is also one of the central characters in "Gang" Bege's Oh My Family Cover Page Serial.

name: Captains
